commands.review_summary:
The PR enables Mistral prompt-cache-key compatibility, separates cache-key support from long-retention forwarding, updates Mistral cached-read pricing/migration, and adds docs plus focused tests.

PR surface: Source +80, Tests +205, Docs 0. Total +285 across 13 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main lacks the Mistral cache-key compat bit while the shared transport gates `prompt_cache_key` on that bit, and current bundled Mistral cached-read costs are zero.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed for the compatibility/default migration decision and current-head proof refresh; there is no safe automated repair target.

target.title: fix(mistral): enable prompt cache key compat
target.body:
Fixes #83709.

## Summary
- mark bundled Mistral OpenAI-compatible models as supporting `prompt_cache_key`
- keep Mistral compat normalization and resolved-compat reuse checks aware of cache-key support without advertising unsupported long retention
- price bundled Mistral cached reads at the documented 10% input-token rate and migrate persisted bundled Mistral model rows that still have old `cacheRead: 0` costs
- document cache-key support separately from long-retention support for OpenAI-compatible providers

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-legacy-config.migrations.test.ts extensions/mistral/api.test.ts extensions/mistral/model-definitions.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-core-normalizers.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-compatibility-base.ts src/commands/doctor-legacy-config.migrations.test.ts docs/reference/prompt-caching.md`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-core-normalizers.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-compatibility-base.ts src/commands/doctor-legacy-config.migrations.test.ts`
- `pnpm docs:list | rg 'prompt-caching|reference'`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior addressed: Mistral model compat did not advertise `supportsPromptCacheKey`, so the shared OpenAI-compatible transport omitted `prompt_cache_key` for Mistral even when a cache key was available. Once cache keys are enabled, cached-token usage also needs a nonzero Mistral `cacheRead` price, including for persisted model rows created before this plugin metadata fix.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on Linux, Node 22-compatible repo tooling, PR head `1dc3236cc325f4d62d7d63f5f1515d2aa46c0650`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx/esm --input-type=module` script importing `applyMistralModelCompat`, `buildMistralModelDefinition`, `buildOpenAICompletionsParams`, and `normalizeCompatibilityConfigValues`, then constructing a Mistral request with `promptCacheKey: "mistral-cache-key"` and running the doctor compatibility normalizer against a persisted Mistral provider with old zero cached-read cost.
- Evidence after fix:
```text
{
  "model": "mistral-large-latest",
  "supportsPromptCacheKey": true,
  "supportsLongCacheRetention": false,
  "prompt_cache_key": "mistral-cache-key",
  "prompt_cache_retention_present": false,
  "cacheReadRate": 0.05,
  "cacheReadCostFor20000Tokens": 0.001
}

{
  "models": [
    {
      "id": "mistral-large-latest",
      "maxTokens": 16384,
      "cacheRead": 0.05
    },
    {
      "id": "MISTRAL-MEDIUM-3-5",
      "maxTokens": 32000,
      "cacheRead": 0.07
    }
  ],
  "changes": [
    "Normalized models.providers.mistral.models[0].maxTokens (262144 → 16384) to avoid Mistral context-window rejects.",
    "Normalized models.providers.mistral.models[0].cost.cacheRead (0 → 0.05) for Mistral prompt-cache billing."
  ]
}
```
- Observed result after fix: The actual OpenClaw Mistral model definition normalizes to `supportsPromptCacheKey: true`, the OpenAI-compatible completions request contains `prompt_cache_key`, long retention remains disabled for Mistral, cached-token cost is nonzero using the bundled default Mistral model rate, and doctor migration updates old bundled Mistral `cacheRead: 0` rows while preserving custom nonzero cached-read costs.
- What was not tested: A live Mistral API cache hit was not run because this environment has no `MISTRAL_API_KEY`; cache-hit timing is also best-effort. The implementation is backed by source-level production-function proof plus focused tests, and pricing follows Mistral's prompt-caching docs.
target.author: alix-007
